proven.claim: Prove: when the asserted condition holds, the function does NOT throw.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 110
; Prove: when the asserted condition holds, the function does NOT throw.
; The contract of asserts.some: must throw if the condition does NOT hold.
; Model:
;   cond = 1  (condition true)
;   thrown = ite(cond = 1, 0, 1)   ; 0 = no throw, 1 = throw
; Negate the property we want to prove: the function throws even though cond = 1.
(declare-const cond Int)   ; 0 = false, 1 = true
(declare-const thrown Int) ; 0 = no‑throw, 1 = throw
; Transition modelling the function body
(assert (= thrown (ite (= cond 1) 0 1)))
; Assume the condition holds
(assert (= cond 1))
; Negated property: a throw occurs despite the condition holding
(assert (= thrown 1))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat (cannot both have cond=1 and thrown=1)

clause_history.clause:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 110
; Prove: when the asserted condition holds, the function does NOT throw.
; The contract of asserts.some: must throw if the condition does NOT hold.
; Model:
;   cond = 1  (condition true)
;   thrown = ite(cond = 1, 0, 1)   ; 0 = no throw, 1 = throw
; Negate the property we want to prove: the function throws even though cond = 1.
(declare-const cond Int)   ; 0 = false, 1 = true
(declare-const thrown Int) ; 0 = no‑throw, 1 = throw
; Transition modelling the function body
(assert (= thrown (ite (= cond 1) 0 1)))
; Assume the condition holds
(assert (= cond 1))
; Negated property: a throw occurs despite the condition holding
(assert (= thrown 1))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat (cannot both have cond=1 and thrown=1)
